flashcards.back: An intelligent agent is a system that perceives its environment and takes actions to achieve its goals.

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between a simple reflex agent and a model-based reflex agent?
flashcards.back: Model-based reflex agents maintain an internal model of the environment, which allows them to make more informed decisions.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the controller in an agent architecture?
flashcards.back: The controller is the decision-making component that processes information from sensors and sends commands to actuators.

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of a goal-based agent?
flashcards.back: Goal-based agents can plan and achieve specific goals, which makes them more effective in certain applications.

flashcards.front: What is the disadvantage of a utility-based agent?
flashcards.back: Utility-based agents require a utility function specification, which can be challenging to define and implement.
